question: What industries does Ansar Mahmood work with?
answer: Ansar works across oil and gas, construction, manufacturing, healthcare, logistics, government, mining, utilities, aviation, and hospitality — across 40+ countries globally. His deep sector experience means he understands your specific hazards, regulatory environment, and operational pressures rather than applying generic safety advice.

question: Can Ansar work with clients outside the UK?
answer: Yes. Ansar works globally — both remotely (virtual consulting, online coaching, remote digital projects) and in-person (on-site consulting, classroom training, project deployments). He has delivered engagements in 40+ countries across the Middle East, Southeast Asia, Africa, Europe, the Americas, and beyond.

question: How does the free consultation work?
answer: The free 30-minute consultation is a no-obligation discovery call via video or phone. You share your current challenges and goals; Ansar gives initial insights and recommends the most appropriate service approach. There is no sales pressure — if there's a fit, we discuss next steps; if not, you'll still leave with useful perspective. Book via the calendar on the Book a Consultation page.

question: What engagement models are available?
answer: Engagements range from standalone project work (a single audit, a specific training course, a dashboard build) to ongoing retainer arrangements (monthly advisory hours, fractional HSE leadership) to comprehensive corporate partner programs covering consulting, training, digital, and AI under a single contract. Full details on the Corporate Solutions page.

question: How quickly can ISO certification be achieved?
answer: Timelines vary based on your organisation's current maturity, size, and number of standards. Typically: ISO 45001 from a low base takes 9–14 months; from a mature HSE system, 4–8 months. Integrated Management Systems (IMS) with ISO 45001 + 14001 + 9001 typically take 12–18 months. A free gap analysis will give you a realistic, costed implementation timeline specific to your situation.

question: Do you help select a certification body?
answer: Yes. Selecting the right certification body for your industry, geography, and client requirements is an important decision. Ansar provides guidance on accredited certification bodies, helps with the Stage 1 and Stage 2 audit preparation, and can liaise with the certification body during the audit process on your behalf.

question: What happens after certification is achieved?
answer: ISO certification requires ongoing surveillance audits (typically annual) and a full recertification audit every 3 years. Ansar provides post-certification maintenance support — keeping your management system current, managing internal audits, facilitating management reviews, and preparing you for surveillance audits so you maintain certification without surprises.

question: What is the NEBOSH exam pass rate?
answer: Ansar maintains a 97%+ first-attempt pass rate across all NEBOSH programs — significantly higher than the global average. This is achieved through structured coaching, regular mock assessments, personalised feedback, and a practical, exam-focused approach to learning that prioritises what examiners are looking for rather than rote memorisation.

question: Can training be delivered to corporate groups?
answer: Yes. Corporate group training is available for teams of 5 to 500+ across classroom, virtual, and blended formats. Courses can be customised to your organisation's sector, hazards, and specific requirements. Bespoke pricing is available for groups. Contact us for a training needs assessment and proposal.

question: Is online / virtual training available?
answer: Yes. All training programs — NEBOSH, IOSH, certification coaching, corporate training, and e-learning — are available fully online via live virtual sessions, on-demand e-learning, or blended combinations. Remote learners receive the same quality of support, resources, and interaction as in-person attendees.

question: What Microsoft 365 subscription do we need for Power Apps / SharePoint?
answer: Microsoft 365 Business Standard or above includes SharePoint, Teams, and basic Power Apps capability. Power BI Pro is required for sharing dashboards with others (approx. £8–10/user/month). Full Power Apps capabilities may require Power Apps per-user plans depending on complexity. Ansar can advise on the most cost-effective licensing approach for your requirements during the discovery call.

question: How long does a Power BI dashboard project take?
answer: A standard HSE dashboard with 3–5 report pages connecting to existing data sources typically takes 2–4 weeks from requirements workshop to delivery. Complex multi-source dashboards with custom data modelling may take 6–8 weeks. The timeline includes requirements, data modelling, design, testing, and user training.

question: Do you provide post-project support and maintenance?
answer: Yes. All digital and AI projects include an initial hypercare period (typically 2–4 weeks) with priority support. Ongoing maintenance plans are available — covering feature additions, data source changes, user access management, and technical troubleshooting — on monthly retainer or as-needed basis.
